perturbation.original_text: "Base Salary" means the Employee's total base salary as of each January 1st exclusive of special payments such as bonuses or commissions, but including any salary reductions made in accordance with Section 125 or 401(k) of the Code.
perturbation.changed_text: "Base Salary" means the Employee's total base salary, inclusive of special payments such as bonuses or commissions, but excluding any salary reductions made in accordance with Section 125 or 401(k) of the Code.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Base Salary'. Originally, bonuses and commissions were excluded. Now, they are included. This impacts the calculation of benefits tied to 'Base Salary' specifically affecting Article IV where the death benefits are calculated as two times the annualized base salary. The death benefit calculation will differ based on which base salary definition is used creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the death of the Employee while this Agreement is in force, the Employee's beneficiary as named in the Beneficiary Designation Form on page 6 (or as it may be amended according to the terms set forth on page 6) for this Agreement will be entitled to receive from the Policy proceeds an amount equal to the lesser of: (a) (two (2) times the Employee's annualized base salary at the time of death as provided by the Employer's payroll department) plus $100,000, reduced by any amount payable under the Employer's group term life insurance plan, or (b) the Net Amount At Risk.
perturbation.justification: The change directly alters the definition of 'Base Salary' by including bonuses and commissions which were previously excluded. The contradicted text (Article IV) uses 'Base Salary' to calculate death benefits. Since the 'Base Salary' definition is changed, the calculation of the death benefit will be inconsistent and creates an in-text contradiction (Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: The Employer will have and may exercise, except as limited hereinafter, all ownership rights in the Policies.
perturbation.changed_text: The Employer will have and may exercise, all ownership rights in the Policies; however, the Employee will have the sole right to decide how the Policy is being exercised without consent from the employer.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding ownership rights. Initially, the Employer had all rights. The modification gives the employee sole discretion on exercising the policy which contradicts the employer's rights. This creates a contradiction in-text with Article XI where the Employer retains possession of the policies and gives the Employer rights as well, creating uncertainty on who has possession, the employer, or the employee, and also who can exercise those rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Employer will keep possession of the Policies. The Employer agrees from time to time to make the policies available to the Employee or to the "Insurer" for the purpose of endorsing or filing any change of beneficiary on the Policies but the Policies will promptly be returned to the Employer.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted the Employer all ownership rights. The changed text gives the Employee the sole right to decide how the Policy is being exercised. This directly contradicts the Employer's ownership rights, as well as Article XI, which states 'The Employer will keep possession of the Policies.' This creates a direct and impactful conflict because it's unclear who ultimately controls and possesses the policies, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be altered, amended or modified, including the addition of any extra policy provisions, by a written instrument signed by the Employer and the Employee.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be altered, amended or modified, including the addition of any extra policy provisions, by a written instrument signed only by the Employer.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the amendment process. Originally, both parties had to agree to modify the agreement. Now, only the Employer's signature is required. This contradicts the initial agreement's statement in Article XIII that includes that the singular includes the plural, meaning that to do any amendment, there should be at least 2 signers, which is contradictory here because this amendment needs only one signer to be approved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Where appropriate in this Agreement, words used in the singular will include the plural and words used in the masculine will include the feminine.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the amendment process to require only the Employer's signature, directly contradicting Article XIII's inclusion of the plural when using the singular. The original agreement requires two parties for any modification, while the changed text allows a single party to unilaterally alter the agreement.
